title: Leads-base record (v5.4.0 pipeline stage 3 output — one record in <source>_leads_base.json)
description: A record in the stable per-source base file <source>_leads_base.json, written by the leads-base writer. It is a debtor-resolved record (debtor_resolved_record.schema.json) decorated with the 18.B aggregation key, the resolved signal_type, and the 13.14 status pair (parcel_resolution_status, enrichment_status). The base file is the ONLY input the aggregator reads (19_aggregator_idempotency_rule.md 19.C); the aggregator never reads its own matched_leads.json output. One record per debtor-resolved event — within-source and cross-source grouping is performed by the aggregator, not pre-collapsed here, so that aggregation stays idempotent from a stable base. v5.4.0 Session 7A (see 17.K) adds the multi-owner block, carried forward from the debtor-resolved record; owner_name stays the required primary display owner and old single-owner records remain valid.

base_record_id.description: Unique id for this base record. Stable across runs given a stable raw_event_id.

signal_type.description: The operator-facing semantic category (18.B), resolved from canonical_doc_type via the county's signal_type_labels map. The third component of the 18.B aggregation key.

aggregation_key.description: The 18.B aggregation key tuple (parcel_id, canonical_doc_type, signal_type). The dedup boundary: records sharing the full key collapse into one signal in matched_leads.json; records differing in any component stay distinct (18.F anti-collapse rule).

parcel_id.description: Resolved parcel id, or null when parcel_resolution_status is UNRESOLVED / REVIEW_REQUIRED. WARNING: 18.B assumes a non-null parcel_id; aggregating null-parcel records by key alone would over-collapse distinct UNRESOLVED properties — see contract finding F-3. The aggregator MUST fall back to a per-record identity (instrument_number) for null-parcel records.

owner_name.description: Resolved debtor per 17 — the PRIMARY display owner — carried from the debtor-resolved record. Never a filer. Backward-compatible: required, unchanged by Session 7A.

filer_entity.description: Filer name carried from the debtor-resolved record (17.E). Null when no filer could be identified — including REVIEW_REQUIRED records produced by the 17 F-5 default rule (no 17.C rule for the doc type). A known-filer pattern may appear here and ONLY here, never in owner_name.

review_reason.description: 17.E routing reason, carried forward. Non-null when parcel_resolution_status is REVIEW_REQUIRED.

parcel_resolution_status.description: 13.14.1 parcel resolution status. REVIEW_REQUIRED is the 17.E debtor-routing value carried forward. Session 7A: this is the downstream needs-review verdict field (the leads-base equivalent of the debtor-resolved record's debtor_resolution_status, per F-1); multi_owner_status never carries a review verdict.

enrichment_status.description: 13.14.1 enrichment status. Tracked independently of parcel_resolution_status — a lead is never dropped because enrichment failed.

confidence_status.description: Rolled-up 08 prime-directive confidence label for this record, computed once by the leads-base writer (the dashboard never computes confidence itself). Added in v5.4.0 Session 3 (see 18.J). DERIVED deterministically from the 08 evidence-ledger entries this record's evidence_ids point to: each entry's status maps to a prime-directive label (Confirmed/Estimated/Possible/Unknown; 'Needs Review' and 'Unsupported' map to Unknown), and confidence_status is the WEAKEST such label — Confirmed > Estimated > Possible > Unknown. A record with no evidence entries is Unknown. A lead is never labelled more confident than its least-supported claim.

instrument_number.description: Recording instrument number. The 18.E within-group dedup unions by this value.

evidence_ids.description: Evidence ledger entry ids backing this record. Mandatory per Build Mode Protocol 02.4.

owners.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A multi-owner block — every distressed owner identified, carried forward from the debtor-resolved record. Co-owners are NEVER dropped. A single-owner record carries exactly one entry, is_primary true. Optional for backward compatibility; old single-owner records that omit the multi-owner block remain valid.

primary_owner_name.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — the primary display owner; equals owner_name when multi_owner_status is SINGLE_OWNER or MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_CLEAR; the 17.E placeholder when MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR.

additional_owner_names.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — names of every non-primary owner. Empty for SINGLE_OWNER.

owner_count.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — count of entries in owners.

multi_owner_status.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — DESCRIPTIVE owner-cardinality status, NOT a review verdict. The needs-review verdict on a leads-base record is parcel_resolution_status (= REVIEW_REQUIRED), the downstream F-1 propagation of the debtor-resolved record's debtor_resolution_status. multi_owner_status has no REVIEW_REQUIRED value; when the primary owner is unclear it is MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR and parcel_resolution_status is independently REVIEW_REQUIRED — the descriptive and verdict fields never contradict.

allOf.description: 13.14.1: ENRICHED requires a RESOLVED parcel — enrichment keys off the parcel identifier.

17.E / v5.4.0 F-5: a REVIEW_REQUIRED parcel_resolution_status carries a non-empty review_reason. filer_entity is NOT required non-null — the 17 F-5 default rule (no 17.C rule for the doc type) routes to REVIEW_REQUIRED with filer_entity null. The pre-F-5 filer_entity constraint here was stale; relaxed in v5.4.0 Session 3 (see 18.J).
v5.4.0 Session 7A — SINGLE_OWNER: exactly one owner, marked primary; owner_count 1; no additional owners.
v5.4.0 Session 7A — MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_CLEAR: more than one owner, exactly one marked primary; owner_count > 1.

v5.4.0 Session 7A — MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR: more than one owner, NONE marked primary; owner_count > 1. multi_owner_status only describes; the verdict is separate — parcel_resolution_status MUST independently be REVIEW_REQUIRED, so the descriptive and verdict fields never contradict.
